channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Bills' Von Miller suspended by NFL for 4 games for violating personal conduct policy
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Buffalo Bills edge rusher Von Miller was suspended four games by the NFL on Tuesday for violating the league's personal conduct policy. The NFL did not reveal the reason for the suspension, which comes as Buffalo (3-1) prepares to play at the Houston Texans on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MLB legend Pete Rose's cause of death revealed
item.description: Pete Rose died on Monday at the age of 83 from hypertensive and atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease, the Clark County Coroner’s Office in Nevada said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dortmund scores 7 while Barcelona, Man City and Arsenal all win in Champions League
item.description: Karim Adeyemi starred as Borussia Dortmund ran riot against Celtic while Barcelona and Manchester City claimed their first wins in the restructured Champions League on Tuesday. Adeyemi scored a first-half hat trick as Dortmund routed Celtic 7-1 at home.
item.dc:creator: CIARÁN FAHEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ex-NFL star Eddie Lacy charged with 'Extreme DUI' in Arizona
item.description: Former NFL running back Eddie Lacy, who was taken in the second round of the 2013 NFL Draft by the Green Bay Packers, was arrested and charged with 4 separate counts of DUI on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NHL season has international flavor with the 4 Nations Face-off as a Winter Olympics appetizer
item.description: Nico Hischier grew up in Switzerland dreaming of playing in the Winter Olympics. “I’ve never gotten to experience that,” he said. The countdown is underway for Hischier, who hopes to join the NHL's best at the Olympics in Milan in February 2026.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Rose on MLB ban for gambling in last interview: 'Other guys will kill somebody and be back in the game'
item.description: Controversial MLB legend Pete Rose made claims that players have "kill somebody" and are allowed to return to baseball in his final TV interview after his death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justin Verlander left off Astros' Wild Card Series roster, but Yordan Alvarez is back
item.description: The Houston Astros left three-time Cy Young Award winner Justin Verlander off the roster for their AL Wild Card Series against Detroit but did include injured slugger Yordan Alvarez. Verlander struggled in his return after missing almost two months with a neck injury this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prep water polo rankings, Oct. 1, 2024: Bay Area News Group Top 10
item.description: High school water polo: Sacred Heart Prep boys, girls stay in top spots. Castro Valley girls enter rankings.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Canilao, The Mercury News

item.title: Lions' Dan Campbell explains why he feels ‘awful’ after Jared Goff’s perfect night
item.description: Detroit Lions head coach Dan Campbell found out speaking to the media after Monday night's win just how good Jared Goff's night was.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gonzaga joins the Pac-12: How the move changes the realignment dynamics for the conference
item.description: The Zags will have an impact on the future of the conference, the school and college basketball on the West Coast.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Legendary Dodgers pitcher, broadcaster Fernando Valenzuela hospitalized
item.description: Dodgers broadcaster Fernando Valenzuela has been hospitalized ahead of the team's playoff run. The popular Spanish-language broadcaster and former Dodger star pitcher stepped away from his playcalling duties last week, though no reason was given at the time, according to the Orange County Register.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Shohei Ohtani and the MLB playoffs, a pairing the world will experience for the 1st time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani was already a superstar with his hitting and pitching accomplishments over six years with the Los Angeles Angels. Yet every October, Ohtani vanished, with the Angels having a losing record and never making the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Henry runs for 199 yards and Jackson has 3 TDs as Ravens rout previously unbeaten Bills 35-10
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Derrick Henry was off and running from the first time he touched the ball. There was no way the Buffalo Bills were stopping the Baltimore Ravens after that.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Americans get biggest road win and capture the Presidents Cup for the 10th straight time
item.description: MONTREAL (AP) — U.S. captain Jim Furyk raised the gold trophy before a team that dressed in red shirts that filled the scoreboard early with red scores. The Presidents Cup is one trophy the Americans own, for two decades and counting.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: College GameDay makes first-ever appearance at Berkeley
item.description: (KRON) -- Since taking its show on the road back in 1993, "College GameDay" has been a huge presence in the college football community by being known to travel across dozens of campuses across the country.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Chastain passes Truex on late restart to win NASCAR Cup Series race at Kansas Speedway
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Ross Chastain finally got to smash a watermelon for the first time this season Sunday at Kansas Speedway. Smashed the hopes of a bunch of playoff drivers trying to earn an automatic berth in the next round, too.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears vs. Rams: Injuries, inactives and keys to Sunday's game at Soldier Field
item.description: Los Angeles Rams (1-2) at Chicago Bears (1-2) Sunday, Noon, FOX UPDATE: The Bears just released their list of inactives on X. Inactive for Sunday's game are WR Velus Jones Jr., FB Khari Blasingame, DL Dominique Robinson, DB Terrell Smith, OL Bill Murray and DL Zacch Pickens.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utes offense struggles in 23-10 loss to Arizona
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The absence of Cam Rising finally caught up to the Utah football team, as the Utes offense produced just one touchdown and one field goal in a 23-10 loss to Arizona Saturday night at Rice-Eccles Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jazz management excited about youth movement
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Back in April, Jazz CEO of Basketball Operations Danny Ainge had his sights on acquiring a big-name player. "Our objective is to find a player or two, and we're we're ready to roll," Ainge said five months ago. "We're ready to go big game hunting.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Shohei Ohtani heads to 1st postseason after Dodgers clinch NL West title with a 7-2 win over Padres
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani is headed to the postseason for the first time in his career after the Los Angeles Dodgers won the NL West on Thursday night by scoring five runs in the seventh inning in a 7-2 win over the second-place San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tom Brady helps Big Ten kickoff nationwide blood drive
item.description: The Big Ten and Abbott have launched a nation-wide blood drive competition to tackle the generational shortage of blood, with the university with the most donations receiving one-million dollars, as the U.S. is currently experiencing one of the worst blood shortages in a generation.
item.dc:creator: Dina Bair, WGN 9

item.title: Brett Favre says Parkinson's diagnosis came after struggles with his right arm
item.description: Brett Favre revealed Tuesday that he had been diagnosed with Parkinson's disease while speaking to a congressional committee.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
